title: Alpine Ecosystem Monitoring
description: Long-term ecological monitoring of vegetation zones, soil carbon stocks, and biodiversity indicators across elevational gradients from 2,500m to 5,500m. Tracks climate-driven phenological shifts using a network of permanent plots and automated sensor stations.

title: Glaciology & Water Security
description: Field-based and remote-sensing research on Himalayan glacier mass balance, glacial lake formation, and downstream water availability. Develops GLOF risk models used by national disaster management authorities in Nepal and Bhutan.

title: AI-Driven Environmental Monitoring
description: Developing agentic AI pipelines for processing satellite imagery, acoustic biodiversity data, and meteorological sensor streams at scale. Applies large language model orchestration to automate environmental change detection and reporting workflows.

title: Traditional Ecological Knowledge
description: Community-participatory documentation of indigenous land management practices, medicinal plant knowledge, and oral ecological histories among mountain communities. Bridges scientific and traditional knowledge systems for conservation planning.

title: Climate Adaptation & Policy
description: Translating field research into evidence-based policy recommendations for mountain communities facing climate change. Collaborates with national governments, ICIMOD, and international bodies to inform adaptation frameworks and protected area management.

title: High-Altitude Biodiversity
description: Systematic surveys of endemic flora and fauna in under-studied high-altitude zones, including snow leopard habitat assessment, bird species range shifts, and rare plant population dynamics. Contributes data to IUCN Red List assessments.
